config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, kalau “demi masyarakat” dipakai tanpa kontrol, sering kali yang paling lemah malah dirugikan. Yang ideal adalah mencari keseimbangan: kepentingan umum dilindungi, tetapi hak dasar orang tetap dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that is exactly how governments and majorities end up trampling people’s basic rights. From a U.S. perspective, our strongest constitutional traditions are built around the idea that individual liberties are a safeguard against collective overreach, even when the majority claims a noble purpose. That said, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case; public safety, public health, and preventing direct harm to others can justify limits. But the burden should be on society to prove that any restriction is necessary and narrowly drawn, not on the individual to justify why their rights matter.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang akhirnya paling berat dirasakan orang kecil, padahal justru hak individu itu pagar agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti keamanan publik atau kesehatan masyarakat yang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi pembatasan itu harus benar-benar perlu dan tidak berlebihan. Jadi, yang paling masuk akal menurut saya bukan mengorbankan individu untuk masyarakat, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan hak dasar tetap menjadi batas yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily that logic can be used to excuse overreach by government or by the majority. Agent A is right that public safety and public health can justify limits, but those limits have to stay exceptional and tightly constrained, not become a blank check for “the common good.” In my view, a society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and then proves, case by case, why any restriction is truly necessary. If you reverse that order, the people with the least power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil dan membuat hak dasar seolah bisa dinegosiasikan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu harus menjadi batas supaya negara atau mayoritas tidak seenaknya menentukan siapa yang harus dikorbankan. Pembatasan hak hanya layak kalau benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, dan tidak berlebihan, misalnya untuk mencegah bahaya nyata bagi orang lain. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan memprioritaskan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan dengan hak individu tetap dilindungi sebagai pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the statement as a general principle, because in the United States we have too much history showing that “society first” can become a convenient excuse for government overreach and majority rule. The strongest version of a free society is not one where individual rights are treated as negotiable whenever the crowd wants something, but one where those rights are the starting point and any limit has to be justified very carefully. Public safety and public health can sometimes override individual freedom, but only in narrow, necessary, and temporary ways. If the burden shifts too far toward society, the people with the least power are usually the ones who get sacrificed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
